topic: graphing inequalities on a number line
latex: Which graph represents all the solutions of the inequality $x < 4$ on the number line?
answer_latex: Number line with open circle at 4 and arrow pointing left (toward negative infinity)

choices_latex.A: Number line with open circle at 4 and arrow pointing left (toward negative infinity)
choices_latex.B: Number line with closed circle at 4 and arrow pointing left (toward negative infinity)
choices_latex.C: Number line with open circles at 0 and 4, with line segment between them and arrows pointing both directions
choices_latex.D: Number line with open circles at -4 and 4, with line segment between them and arrows pointing both directions

diagram_notes: Four number lines labeled A-D, each showing marks at -4, 0, and 4. Option A: open circle at 4 with arrow extending left to negative infinity. Option B: closed/filled circle at 4 with arrow extending left to negative infinity. Option C: open circles at both 0 and 4 with a line segment between them and arrows on both ends. Option D: open circles at both -4 and 4 with a line segment between them and arrows on both ends.
